level_signals: Works directly with CTOs, C-level audiences, and engineering leaders
Operates with high autonomy in ambiguous environments
Background as technical founder, product founder, or technical PM implied

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong technical fluency and comfort working directly with engineers.
Strong product instincts, especially around identifying customer needs and translating them into product opportunities.
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills.
Ability to present product strategy and deployment vision clearly to senior customer audiences.
Comfort operating across strategy, execution, and hands-on problem solving.
Bias toward action, ownership, and unblocking.
Act as the product lead inside strategic customer accounts, helping customers adopt and expand their use of OpenAI products.
Work hands-on with integrations, MCPs, tooling, and lightweight technical workflows when needed to unblock an account.
You've operated in highly ambiguous environments and are comfortable being the connective tissue between customers, engineering, GTM, and product.
You are highly credible with technical audiences and can work effectively with CTOs, senior engineers, and developer experience teams.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You likely have a background as a technical founder, product founder, technical PM, solutions or product lead, or in another role that combines technical depth with product judgment and customer-facing execution.

scope.surface: Codex — enterprise customer deployment and expansion for OpenAI products
primary_metrics_inferred: Customer product adoption depth and breadth
core_responsibilities: Act as the product lead inside strategic customer accounts, helping customers adopt and expand their use of OpenAI products
Identify opportunities for deeper deployment, new integrations, and broader product usage across teams and workflows
Build trusted relationships with technical stakeholders, engineering leaders, and executives, including representing product vision to C-level audiences
Partner closely with Deployment Engineers and GTM teams to drive successful implementations and long-term account growth
Translate customer needs and technical blockers into clear product feedback and execution paths for internal teams
Work hands-on with integrations, MCPs, tooling, and lightweight technical workflows when needed to unblock an account
Own lightweight PM work needed to support enterprise deployment, then hand off the right insights and opportunities to core product teams

green_flags: Role has a clear, well-defined product surface (Codex / enterprise deployment)
Cross-functional ownership explicitly described (Deployment Engineers, GTM, core product)
Encourages non-traditional backgrounds (technical founders, solutions leads)
Role sits at the strategic intersection of product, technical deployment, and customer strategy — high visibility
red_flags: Location and remote/hybrid policy not stated anywhere in the JD
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
No seniority level or years of experience stated — hard to gauge leveling
JD title includes 'Codex' but body never mentions Codex specifically — ambiguous product scope

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from C-level stakeholder interaction and ambiguous environment framing)
scope.business_model (inferred from enterprise customer deployment context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
ambiguities: Role title references 'Codex' but the JD body never mentions Codex by name — unclear if this is specifically the Codex product or a broader OpenAI enterprise deployment role
No location, remote policy, or office requirement stated anywhere in the JD
Nice-to-have background signals use 'You likely have' language which is ambiguous — treated as soft/nice-to-have rather than hard requirement
